summary: Validate topology-backed map recovery before M7 content-lock reconsideration
acceptance_criteria: Prove the default player-facing map is driven by explicit district polygons and route graph, not rectangular-grid topology, hex axial/cube coordinates, sequential id order, or row/column/regular-lattice neighbor shortcuts.
Prove map/list/task rail/inspector focus and route preview reason codes remain synchronized across the core playable path.
Prove deterministic path previews, tie-breakers, topology hash, save/load or structured rejection behavior, and browser/node replay remain stable.

Include negative tests for invalid adjacency, missing route endpoint, blocked route reason, disconnected graph, duplicate route capacity use, visual-neighbor-without-route, visually-distant-explicit-route, geometry perturbation without path change, and legacy grid/hex/lattice leakage.
Produce screenshot/manual-smoke evidence at 1440x900 and 1280x720 with clean console output and player-visible route/legend explanation.
After acceptance and main integration, reopen M7-CONTENT-LOCK-ACCEPTANCE-001 for product-owner reconsideration; do not approve content lock autonomously.
required_tests: focused topology validation negative tests, including anti-grid/lattice leakage tests
manual-smoke notes with screenshots and console-clean assertions
notes: This validation blocks formal M7 content lock acceptance after the product-owner topology rejection.
It must not start M7-EXIT-VALIDATION-001 or claim content lock approval.
